items.why: Students confuse fat- and water-soluble vitamins. Mnemonic: ADEK fat-soluble (stored in body fat - can build up to toxic levels). B and C water-soluble (excess excreted in urine).

items.q: Why does food provenance matter to consumers?
items.correct: It affects environmental impact, quality, ethics, and supports local economies.
items.random_wrong: It tells you the calorie content.
items.why: Students reduce food choice to price. Provenance (origin) raises questions of food miles, fair trade, animal welfare, seasonal availability, and supporting local producers - all consumer concerns.

items.q: Which vitamin is most likely to be deficient in a poorly planned vegan diet?
items.why: Students guess vitamin C (it's the famous one), but plant foods are rich in C. B12 is found almost exclusively in animal products, so vegans need fortified foods or supplements.

items.q: Eatwell Guide '5-a-day' portion size is approximately:
items.why: Correct answer: 80 g. “40 g” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: UK adult salt target per day is:
items.why: Correct answer: ≤6 g. “≤2 g” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If 5 × 80 g portions are recommended, total recommended fruit/veg in grams per day is:
items.why: The real figure is 400. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
